hypothesis: If the training data is biased toward frequent reaction templates and products that are structurally similar to the test set, then both template-based and template-free retrosynthesis models will achieve high top-k and round-trip accuracy in-distribution (USPTO-retro, Pistachio ID) but suffer large drops on out-of-distribution reactions with unseen templates (Pistachio OOD), because their predictions depend on memorizing common templates and close analogs rather than learning generalizable disconnection rules.
